questions.stem: These books ______ to the library tomorrow afternoon.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语'These books'是动作'return'的承受者，因此需要用被动语态。时间状语'tomorrow afternoon'表明是将来时，所以用'will be returned'。A是主动语态原形，B是过去式，D是现在完成时主动语态，均不符合。解题技巧：看到tomorrow等将来时间标志词，且主语是物时，考虑will be done结构。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —______ you ______ the film before? —Yes, I ______ it last week.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。问句中的'before'（之前）是现完的标志词，强调过去动作对现在的影响，用'Have you seen'；答句中的'last week'（上周）是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时'saw'。A选项助动词Did后应接动词原形，但答句时态错误；C选项答句用了现完，与last week矛盾；D选项问句时态错误。口诀：有before现完成，有last week用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: This is the museum ______ we visited last summer holiday.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'the museum'是物，且在从句中作visited的宾语，因此用which或that。where在从句中作地点状语，who指人，when指时间。判断技巧：看先行词是人还是物，再看关系词在从句中充当什么成分。本句visited后缺宾语，museum是物，所以选which。
questions.label: 语法题

options: A. where is the nearest hospital
B. where the nearest hospital is
C. where was the nearest hospital
D. where does the nearest hospital lie
questions.explanation: 这道题考查宾语从句的语序。宾语从句必须使用陈述句语序（主语+谓语），不能用疑问句语序。A、C、D都是疑问句语序（is/was/does在主语前），只有B是陈述句语序（the nearest hospital is）。注意：宾语从句的时态要与主句呼应，但本题主句Could you tell是委婉请求，不是过去时，从句根据实际情况用一般现在时即可。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It is ______ to play computer games for too long. It may hurt your eyes.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查词性转换。系动词is后需要接形容词作表语。harm是名词/动词，harmful是形容词（有害的），harmless是形容词（无害的），harmed是过去分词。根据句意'玩电脑游戏太久是有害的'，选harmful。构词法技巧：-ful后缀通常表示'充满...的'，-less表示'没有...的'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: Please ______ the lights when you leave the classroom to save electricity.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查动词短语辨析。turn on打开，turn off关闭，turn up调大（音量），turn down调小（音量）/拒绝。根据语境'leave the classroom'和'save electricity'，应该是关灯，选turn off。解题技巧：结合上下文逻辑，离开教室要省电，自然是关闭电器。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The old man lives ______ but he doesn't feel ______ because he has many neighbors to talk with.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查alone与lonely的辨析。alone是副词，强调客观上独自一人；lonely是形容词，强调主观上感到孤独。第一空修饰动词lives，用副词alone；第二空feel是系动词，后接形容词lonely作表语。句意：老人独自居住，但不感到孤独。技巧：alone客观描述，lonely主观感受。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 这道题考查固定搭配。make a plan是固定短语，意为'制定计划'。find找到，take带走，give给，均不与plan构成搭配。解题技巧：完形填空中常考动词与名词的固定搭配，需要积累常见组合如make a plan/decision/difference等。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an exciting science fair in the gym. All the students from Grade 8 were looking forward to showing their creative ideas and inventions. Our science teacher, Mr. Chen, encouraged us to think about green technology and recycling. I decided to make something both interesting and useful for the environment.

After thinking for several days, I (8)______ a detailed plan to create a small machine that could turn waste paper into new notebooks. First, I collected old newspapers and magazines from my classmates and neighbors. I also watched many online videos to learn how paper recycling works. Then, I spent three weekends (9)______ it with my father's help in our garage. The hardest part was making the machine work smoothly without breaking down. Sometimes the paper got stuck inside the gears, and I had to take everything apart and fix it again and again. It was frustrating and tiring, but I didn't give up because I wanted to prove that waste could be treasure.

On the day of the fair, many students and teachers (10)______ my project and asked lots of questions. They were surprised that waste paper could become clean and usable notebooks again so easily. My teacher said this invention would help reduce pollution greatly if it (11)______ by more people in the future. Finally, I won the first prize. I felt proud not just because of the prize, (12)______ because I did something good for our planet through my own efforts.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查非谓语动词。spend time (in) doing sth是固定句型，意为'花费时间做某事'，其中in可以省略，后面必须接动词的-ing形式。只有building是动名词形式。其他选项都是介词，不能直接跟在spend three weekends后表示花费时间做某事。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an exciting science fair in the gym. All the students from Grade 8 were looking forward to showing their creative ideas and inventions. Our science teacher, Mr. Chen, encouraged us to think about green technology and recycling. I decided to make something both interesting and useful for the environment.

After thinking for several days, I (8)______ a detailed plan to create a small machine that could turn waste paper into new notebooks. First, I collected old newspapers and magazines from my classmates and neighbors. I also watched many online videos to learn how paper recycling works. Then, I spent three weekends (9)______ it with my father's help in our garage. The hardest part was making the machine work smoothly without breaking down. Sometimes the paper got stuck inside the gears, and I had to take everything apart and fix it again and again. It was frustrating and tiring, but I didn't give up because I wanted to prove that waste could be treasure.

On the day of the fair, many students and teachers (10)______ my project and asked lots of questions. They were surprised that waste paper could become clean and usable notebooks again so easily. My teacher said this invention would help reduce pollution greatly if it (11)______ by more people in the future. Finally, I won the first prize. I felt proud not just because of the prize, (12)______ because I did something good for our planet through my own efforts.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查语境理解。根据上下文，在科技展当天，学生和老师应该是来'参观'项目，asked lots of questions也印证了这一点。look for寻找（强调过程），laugh at嘲笑（与后文 surprised和won prize矛盾），worry about担心（不符合语境）。visit参观，符合展览场景。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an exciting science fair in the gym. All the students from Grade 8 were looking forward to showing their creative ideas and inventions. Our science teacher, Mr. Chen, encouraged us to think about green technology and recycling. I decided to make something both interesting and useful for the environment.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查被动语态。主语it指代invention，与use之间是被动关系（发明被使用），因此要用被动语态be+过去分词。if引导的条件状语从句，主句would help是过去将来时，从句用一般过去时，但此处by more people表明被动，且表示客观事实，用一般现在时被动is used更合适（主将从现原则的变化）。was used虽也是被动，但if虚拟语气中，与将来事实相反用过去式或should/were to，此处表示真实条件，用一般现在时表将来。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an exciting science fair in the gym. All the students from Grade 8 were looking forward to showing their creative ideas and inventions. Our science teacher, Mr. Chen, encouraged us to think about green technology and recycling. I decided to make something both interesting and useful for the environment.

I felt proud not just because of the prize, (12)______ because I did something good for our planet through my own efforts.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查固定搭配。not...but...是固定结构，意为'不是……而是……'。前文有not just because，后文对应but because，表示'不仅因为……而是因为……'。and用于并列，or表选择，so表结果，均不构成此搭配。

options: A. Here are some simple ways to begin.
B. However, don't drink too much coffee.
C. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
E. It is better than sugary drinks.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查段落主旨句。空格位于段首，后文用First引出第一个建议，说明此处是总起句，引出下文的建议。A选项'这里有一些简单的开始方法'符合总分结构，承上启下。B谈咖啡，C谈睡眠，D谈活动量，E谈饮料，都是具体细节，不适合作为引出First的总起句。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students want to stay healthy and energetic, but they often don't know how to start or what to do first. (13)______ First, eating a good breakfast is very important for teenagers. It gives you enough energy for the whole morning and helps your brain work better. Try to eat eggs, milk and fresh fruit instead of junk food like chips or hamburgers.

Second, remember to exercise regularly. You don't need to run marathons or join expensive gyms to stay fit. (14)______ Walking to school, riding a bike for 20 minutes, or playing basketball with friends every day can make a big difference to your health over time.

(15)______ Many students nowadays stay up late to play computer games or watch short videos on their phones. However, doctors say teenagers need at least 8 hours of sleep to grow well and stay focused. If you don't sleep enough, you will feel tired and sleepy in class.

Finally, drink plenty of water throughout the day. (16)______ Soft drinks and energy drinks may taste good but they contain too much sugar which is bad for your teeth and body. Pure water is always the best choice for keeping your body hydrated and healthy.

Follow these simple tips consistently, and you will soon feel better, look better, and study better at school.

options: A. Here are some simple ways to begin.
B. However, don't drink too much coffee.
C. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
E. It is better than sugary drinks.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查逻辑衔接。前文说'你不需要跑马拉松或加入昂贵的健身房'，后文说'步行上学、骑20分钟自行车或打篮球'，这些都是小运动量的例子。D选项'小型活动就足够了'承上启下，解释不需要剧烈运动，日常小活动即可。A是总起句已用过，B、C、E话题不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

It gives you enough energy for the whole morning and helps your brain work better. Try to eat eggs, milk and fresh fruit instead of junk food like chips or hamburgers.

Second, remember to exercise regularly. You don't need to run marathons or join expensive gyms to stay fit. (14)______ Walking to school, riding a bike for 20 minutes, or playing basketball with friends every day can make a big difference to your health over time.

(15)______ Many students nowadays stay up late to play computer games or watch short videos on their phones. However, doctors say teenagers need at least 8 hours of sleep to grow well and stay focused. If you don't sleep enough, you will feel tired and sleepy in class.

Finally, drink plenty of water throughout the day. (16)______ Soft drinks and energy drinks may taste good but they contain too much sugar which is bad for your teeth and body. Pure water is always the best choice for keeping your body hydrated and healthy.

Follow these simple tips consistently, and you will soon feel better, look better, and study better at school.

options: A. Here are some simple ways to begin.
B. However, don't drink too much coffee.
C. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
E. It is better than sugary drinks.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查段落主题句。本段主要讲睡眠的重要性，提到stay up late和need at least 8 hours of sleep。C选项'此外，获得足够的睡眠是必要的'作为段首主题句恰当。In addition表示递进，承接前面的First和Second。其他选项话题与睡眠无关。

questions.explanation: 这道题考查指代衔接。前文说'喝大量的水'，后文说'软饮料可能味道好但含糖太多'。E选项'它比含糖饮料更好'中的It指代water，承上启下，引出后文对软饮料的对比说明。B选项也谈饮料，但用However转折语气不如E选项顺承自然，且咖啡不是本段重点。

questions.stem: What happens when sea animals eat plastic according to the passage?
options: B. They get sick or die.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查细节理解。根据文章第一段最后一句'When this happens, the animals can get sick or even die because the plastic blocks their stomachs.'（当这种情况发生时，动物会生病甚至死亡，因为塑料堵塞了它们的胃。）可知答案是B。A、C、D均与原文不符。解题技巧：细节题要找准关键词（sea animals eat plastic），回原文定位具体句子。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Our oceans are facing a serious environmental problem that affects everyone on Earth. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste enter the sea through rivers and coastlines. This causes great danger to sea animals and plants. Many turtles, fish and birds mistake plastic bags for jellyfish or other food and eat them accidentally. When this happens, the animals can get sick or even die because the plastic blocks their stomachs.

However, some clever young people around the world are finding creative ways to solve this growing problem. In Australia, a group of teenagers invented a special machine that cleans plastic from rivers before it reaches the ocean. The machine works like a large vacuum cleaner. It sucks up rubbish while letting water and small fish pass through safely. This prevents tons of plastic from ever reaching the sea.

Another good idea comes from the Netherlands. Scientists there created something called a bubble barrier—a wall of air bubbles that pushes plastic waste to one side of the river. This simple but effective method stops the waste from floating into the open sea. Amazingly, fish can swim through the bubbles easily without any harm, but plastic pieces cannot get past the barrier.

These wonderful inventions show that young people can really make a difference in protecting nature. By keeping our rivers clean, we also protect the oceans and all the creatures living there. Remember, everyone can help by using fewer plastic bags and bottles in daily life. Small changes in our habits can lead to big improvements for the environment.

questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage?
options: A. All sea animals like eating plastic.
B. The bubble barrier kills fish.
C. Teenagers invented useful machines to help the environment.
D. Plastic waste is not a serious problem now.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查推理判断。根据第二段'In Australia, a group of teenagers invented a special machine...'可知青少年发明了有用的机器帮助环境，C正确。A错误，动物是mistake...for（误以为）而非喜欢；B错误，第三段说fish can swim through the bubbles easily without any harm；D错误，文章开头就说facing a serious environmental problem。推理题要基于原文信息，不能过度推断。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: The underlined phrase 'sucks up' in Paragraph 2 probably means ______ in Chinese.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查词义猜测。根据前文'works like a large vacuum cleaner'（像大型吸尘器一样工作），吸尘器的工作原理是吸入，因此suck up意为'吸走'。后文also提到letting water and small fish pass through（让水和小鱼通过），进一步说明机器像吸尘器一样吸走垃圾但让其他东西通过。词义猜测技巧：利用上下文线索，如比喻词like，以及同段内的解释说明。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Our oceans are facing a serious environmental problem that affects everyone on Earth. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste enter the sea through rivers and coastlines. This causes great danger to sea animals and plants. Many turtles, fish and birds mistake plastic bags for jellyfish or other food and eat them accidentally. This simple but effective method stops the waste from floating into the open sea. Amazingly, fish can swim through the bubbles easily without any harm, but plastic pieces cannot get past the barrier.

These wonderful inventions show that young people can really make a difference in protecting nature. By keeping our rivers clean, we also protect the oceans and all the creatures living there. Remember, everyone can help by using fewer plastic bags and bottles in daily life. Small changes in our habits can lead to big improvements for the environment.

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. The Beauty of the Ocean
C. Protecting Our Oceans: Problems and Solutions
questions.explanation: 这道题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章结构为：第一段提出问题（海洋塑料污染），第二、三段提出解决方案（澳大利亚青少年的发明、荷兰的气泡屏障），第四段总结。C选项'保护我们的海洋：问题与解决方案'准确概括全文。A只谈美丽，未涉及污染和解决；B偏离主题；D只是文中一个例子。主旨题技巧：寻找涵盖全文主要内容的选项，既要有problem也要有solution。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Our oceans are facing a serious environmental problem that affects everyone on Earth. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste enter the sea through rivers and coastlines. This causes great danger to sea animals and plants. Many turtles, fish and birds mistake plastic bags for jellyfish or other food and eat them accidentally. When this happens, the animals can get sick or even die because the plastic blocks their stomachs.

However, some clever young people around the world are finding creative ways to solve this growing problem. In Australia, a group of teenagers invented a special machine that cleans plastic from rivers before it reaches the ocean. The machine works like a large vacuum cleaner. It sucks up rubbish while letting water and small fish pass through safely. This prevents tons of plastic from ever reaching the sea.

Another good idea comes from the Netherlands. Scientists there created something called a bubble barrier—a wall of air bubbles that pushes plastic waste to one side of the river. This simple but effective method stops the waste from floating into the open sea. Amazingly, fish can swim through the bubbles easily without any harm, but plastic pieces cannot get past the barrier.

These wonderful inventions show that young people can really make a difference in protecting nature. By keeping our rivers clean, we also protect the oceans and all the creatures living there. Remember, everyone can help by using fewer plastic bags and bottles in daily life. Small changes in our habits can lead to big improvements for the environment.
